architecture.summary: SONAR-LLM is a decoder-only Transformer that autoregressively predicts sequences of sentence-level embeddings in the frozen SONAR embedding space (1024-dimensional). At each step, given a prefix of sentence embeddings, the model outputs a continuous vector representing the next sentence embedding. This predicted embedding is decoded back to tokens via the frozen SONAR decoder using teacher-forcing, and token-level cross-entropy loss is computed against the ground-truth token sequence. Gradients flow backward through the decoder to train only the embedding-prediction transformer, keeping SONAR encoder and decoder frozen. Generation halts when predicted embedding similarity to a learned "End of sequence" token exceeds a threshold or a sentence limit is reached.

biblio.title: SONAR-LLM: Autoregressive Transformer that Thinks in Sentence Embeddings and Speaks in Tokens

claims.statement: SONAR-LLM achieves a strong scaling exponent of 0.596, comparable to other embedding-based models, with R-squared exceeding 0.98 in scaling law fits across model sizes from 39M to 900M parameters.

In GPT-4o evaluations on 512 generated stories, SONAR-LLM 900M outperforms both diffusion-based and MSE-based LCM variants, achieving grammatical correctness of 7.1, creativity of 5.2, coherence of 6.0, and plot consistency of 5.2, compared to diffusion LCM (grammar 3.9, creativity 5.0, coherence 2.9, plot 2.8) and MSE LCM (grammar 2.1, creativity 3.0, coherence 1.3, plot 1.2).

SONAR-LLM 900M matches LLM performance on ROUGE-L with short prefixes (39.4 vs. 39.4) and achieves competitive METEOR (32.9 vs. 34.2). With longer context, SONAR-LLM attains comparable ROUGE-L (35.2 vs. 35.7), substantially outperforming MSE LCM (26.1 ROUGE-L) and diffusion LCM (31.2 ROUGE-L) on short prefixes.

On XSum, SONAR-LLM 1.3B achieves ROUGE-L of 18.5(0.7) and METEOR of 14.5(0.5). On CNN/DailyMail, SONAR-LLM 1.3B achieves ROUGE-L of 15.5(0.7) and METEOR of 10.0(0.4), substantially outperforming diffusion LCM (XSum: 12.0 ROUGE-L; CNN/DM: 10.1 ROUGE-L) and MSE LCM (XSum: 11.6 ROUGE-L; CNN/DM: 7.8 ROUGE-L).

On RULER needle-in-a-haystack task with unfrozen decoder, exact-match accuracy improves from approximately 45% to 82% (nearly doubling), indicating clear benefit for symbol-heavy long-context retrieval tasks.

For sequences beyond approximately 4096 tokens, SONAR-LLM surpasses standard token-level LLM in theoretical inference efficiency; up to 1 million tokens, SONAR-LLM shows almost linear growth in FLOPs (vs. quadratic for LLM), because it operates on compressed sequences of sentence embeddings scaled by the inverse square of average sentence length (60 tokens).

Unfreezing the SONAR decoder during pretraining consistently reduced validation cross-entropy by more than 10% but did not translate into statistically significant gains in downstream text quality on summarization benchmarks (XSum and CNN/DailyMail).

All models were trained using a cosine learning rate scheduler with experiments on two learning rates (1e-4 and 5e-4); SONAR-LLM performed optimally with 1e-4 while LLM, MSE-based LCM, and diffusion-based LCM performed better with 5e-4, indicating different optimization sensitivities across architectures.
